problem.instruction: Read the conversation in <A> and the passage in <B>, and follow the directions.
problem.passage:
<A>
(Two teachers, Mr. Lee and Ms. Kim, recently scored students' speaking assessments. They later discussed the scoring process and Mr. Lee reflected on his scoring experiences in his journals.)

Mr. Lee: As I was reviewing my ratings, I noticed that they were staying consistent throughout the scoring process.
Ms. Kim: Good, it's actually hard to keep the same perspective when grading multiple students. But you mean you found actual similarities in your scores for the same students over time?
Mr. Lee: Yes, exactly. I think I might have benefitted from reviewing my previous scores before re-evaluating anyone's performance to see if I'm staying consistent.
Ms. Kim: That makes sense. You know, I've noticed that we have some scoring differences between us on certain criteria.
Mr. Lee: Right. I normally give a score of 10 if students have natural flow even though they may demonstrate some errors in grammar or vocabulary. How about you?
Ms. Kim: Oh, I've constantly made efforts to adhere to our scoring criteria, and I give a perfect score only when they speak without any errors or hesitation.
Mr. Lee: All right. Now I can see why we have different scoring results and it makes me think — these different scoring results could send mixed messages to students.
Ms. Kim: I agree. Let's review our criteria and stick to following our rubric.
Mr. Lee: Sure. That would be fairer for the students.

<B>
Mr. Lee's Reflective Journal
After today's grading session, I reviewed my scores and luckily noticed consistency in my ratings for the same students across different sessions. However, after talking with Ms. Kim, I realized that we provided different scores for the same students. I'm concerned this could lead to some confusion if they receive different scores based on which teacher assesses them. I think it would be helpful if Ms. Kim and I could go over the rubric together to ensure a more unified scoring approach.

Fill in the blanks with the TWO most appropriate words.
Based on <A> and <B>, Mr. Lee is concerned about the lack of ________ in the scoring process. His concern is not about the consistency of rating by a single rater but about the consistency of rating by different raters.

blanks.label: ①
blanks.context: lack of ___ in the scoring process (two words)

steps.label: 발문 분석
steps.explanation: 두 단어(TWO most appropriate words)로 빈칸을 채워야 한다. 빈칸은 'lack of ___'로, 채점 과정에서 부족한 것을 묻는다.

steps.label: 지문 근거 확인
steps.explanation: <A>에서 Mr. Lee는 자신의 점수 일관성(intra-rater)은 확인했지만, Ms. Kim과 동일 학생에 대해 서로 다른 점수를 부여했음을 발견한다. <B>에서도 'we provided different scores for the same students'라고 명시되어 있다. 즉, 서로 다른 채점자 간 일관성이 문제다.

steps.label: 발문 단서 적용
steps.explanation: 발문은 'not about the consistency of rating by a single rater but about the consistency of rating by different raters'라고 명확히 힌트를 준다. 단일 채점자 일관성(intra-rater reliability)이 아니라 복수 채점자 간 일관성(inter-rater reliability)이 부족하다는 것이다.

steps.label: 확정 근거
steps.explanation:
inter-rater reliability는 두 명 이상의 채점자가 동일 수행에 대해 일관된 점수를 부여하는 정도를 의미한다. Mr. Lee와 Ms. Kim이 서로 다른 기준으로 채점한 상황이 이에 해당한다.
→ 근거: <B> 'we provided different scores for the same students'
→ 발문: 'consistency of rating by different raters' = inter-rater reliability

steps.label: 전략 메모
steps.explanation:
출제 패턴: Mr. Lee의 자기 일관성(intra-rater) vs. 두 교사 간 불일치(inter-rater)를 대비시켜 혼동을 유도. 발문에 'not about the consistency of rating by a single rater but about the consistency of rating by different raters'라는 명시적 힌트가 있으므로 intra-를 배제하고 inter-rater reliability를 선택한다.
함정: 'Mr. Lee가 자신의 점수가 일관됐다'는 인트라 신뢰도 예시이지 정답이 아님. 정답은 두 교사 간 불일치 → inter-rater reliability.

key_terms.definition_en: The degree to which the results of scoring by two or more scorers are consistent.
key_terms.definition_ko: 두 명 이상의 채점자가 동일한 수행을 채점할 때 점수가 일치하는 정도.
key_terms.exam_usage: 서로 다른 교사가 동일 학생에게 다른 점수를 부여하는 상황에서 inter-rater reliability 부족으로 출제

key_terms.definition_en: The degree to which the results of scoring performed by a single scorer are consistent.
key_terms.definition_ko: 동일 채점자가 같은 수행을 반복 채점할 때 점수가 일치하는 정도.
key_terms.exam_usage: Mr. Lee가 자신의 점수가 일관됨을 확인한 것이 intra-rater reliability에 해당

scoring_criteria.criterion: ① inter-rater reliability (두 단어 모두 정확히 기재)

references.author: Brown, H. D.
references.title: Language Assessment: Principles and Classroom Practices
references.excerpt: Inter-rater reliability refers to the degree to which two or more independent raters agree in their scoring of the same performance. When different teachers apply the same rubric differently — one accepting minor errors as fluent while another requiring error-free speech for a top score — inter-rater reliability is compromised. This is distinguished from intra-rater reliability, the consistency of a single rater across multiple scoring sessions.

references.author: Brown, H. D. & Abeywickrama, P.
references.title: Language Assessment: Principles and Classroom Practices (3rd ed.)
references.excerpt: Reliability requires that a test yield consistent results under consistent conditions. Inter-rater reliability is particularly important in performance assessment, where subjective judgment plays a role. To maximize inter-rater reliability, raters should be trained together, anchor papers should be established, and detailed scoring criteria should be applied uniformly. A discrepancy between raters' scores signals a need for rubric clarification and scorer calibration.

source.concept_explanation: Reliability in language assessment refers to the consistency of test scores. Inter-rater reliability (채점자 간 신뢰도) measures agreement between two or more raters scoring the same performance. It is distinguished from intra-rater reliability (채점자 내 신뢰도), which refers to a single rater's consistency across scoring sessions. To improve inter-rater reliability, teachers should calibrate their scoring by reviewing rubric criteria together and conducting joint scoring sessions before independent assessment.
